methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Xcimer Energy, founded by Conner Galloway and Alexander Valys, is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers (including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying a $200M valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yields a median implied valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $128M (low) to $230M (high), with a median of $175M. The stage-progression method and peer-median method converge most closely in the $150M–$200M band, suggesting the center of gravity lies near $175M. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next funding round, relative technology maturity versus peers, and capital efficiency versus the broader climate-tech cohort. The $128M–$230M range reflects appropriate skepticism around both methods' assumptions at this early stage.
